environment.notes: Single-threaded WASM only (no COOP/COEP on python -m http.server).

fp32_webgpu.error_message: [WebGPU] Kernel "[MatMul] /text_encoder/0/layers.0/conv_module1/out_proj/MatMul" failed. Error: shared dimension does not match.
fp32_webgpu.notes: Fails at first conv module's output projection MatMul on both text_encoder runs. ORT-Web WebGPU kernel bug — same ONNX runs fine on CPU EP per Spike A.

int8_webgpu.error_message: Non-zero status code returned while running MatMulInteger node. Name:'/text_encoder/0/layers.0/conv_module1/out_proj/MatMul_quant' Status Message: matmul_helper.h:41 Compute left_num_dims and right_num_dims must be >= 1
int8_webgpu.notes: Different error but same op location. Confirms this is a WebGPU-specific kernel bug affecting both quantized and unquantized variants.

fp32_wasm.notes: Single-threaded WASM. Multi-threaded (with COOP/COEP) should be 3-4x faster.

int8_wasm.notes: Load time ~2x faster than FP32; compute roughly equivalent because WASM doesn't natively accelerate INT8 (dequant overhead per op).

summary.webgpu_failure_reason: ORT-Web 1.19.2 kernel bugs at conv_module output projection MatMul for both FP32 and INT8 paths
summary.wasm_extrapolation_notes: Single-threaded WASM is 0.57-0.59x real-time. Multi-threaded WASM (COOP/COEP + SharedArrayBuffer) typically 3-4x faster → ~2x real-time = shippable.
summary.recommended_deploy_path: INT8 + WASM + COOP/COEP headers initially; keep WebGPU as in-progress perf path.
summary.decision: PROCEED to Phase 1. Browser-native feasibility confirmed.
